subtitle: The Queens beach Robert Moses built as a working-class civic monument, and the eastern bays a gay New York made its own for eighty years against the rules of the place.
spike_statement: A Moses-era civic beach whose far eastern bays became the oldest gay beach in New York, claimed since the 1940s.

levers.photo_tone: flat Atlantic light on faded brick, octagonal bathhouse towers, salt-bleached concrete, the eastern dune line
